title: Limit watcher: auto-resume agents after usage-limit resets
description: Operator-requested (2026-07-02): a deterministic background watcher that monitors agents/status.json for out_of_tokens entries with resume_after timestamps, auto-resumes those agents via visible hcom tabs when their limit window resets, detects agents that stopped silently without recording status, and logs durable events. Non-LLM polling script; implements the automation half of notes/limit-exhaustion-protocol.md.
acceptance_criteria: Watcher nudges an agent at most once per resume_after window (no spawn loops)
All resumes use --terminal wezterm-tab, never headless, per operator visibility rule
Unparseable resume_after values are skipped with a durable warning, not guessed
Silent stops (agent gone from hcom without status.json update) produce a durable event
Test registered in run_tests.sh and full suite passes
objective: Close the last gap in limit-exhaustion recovery: a watcher process that is not itself subject to usage limits, so agent resume no longer depends on a dying agent scheduling its own alarm.
forbidden_changes: No headless agent spawning (operator visibility rule)
No LLM/Ollama inference in the polling loop
No writes to agents/status.json entries owned by live agents (watcher only reads status; it writes its own state file)
No automatic retry storms: one nudge per agent per resume window
expected_artifacts: protocol note updated to reference the watcher
watcher running in background, verified alive
risk: Medium-low: the watcher spawns paid agent sessions automatically at reset time. Mitigations: once-per-window state tracking, visible terminal tabs, dry-run mode, durable event per action, easily killable via pidfile.
